verse_specific_anchors: the four-fold compound vihāya kāmān / niḥspṛha / nirmama / nirahaṅkāra is preserved verbatim with each component glossed; this exact four-fold structure is unique to 2.71

the engineering layer explicitly identifies the third and fourth (nirmama / nirahaṅkāra) as the engineering-pathology-naming components — anchored on the verse's specific lexical structure rather than general 'be humble' advice

the 'I am the engineer who shipped this' / 'this is MY design' framing is licensed by the privative compound nirmama (nir + mama, 'without mine') and ahaṅkāra (the I-maker), and is not reducible to general non-attachment teaching
objection: The analog could not equally apply to 2.55 (Arjuna's question about the sthita-prajña), 2.56 (the sage unmoved by sukha-duḥkha), 2.57 (the sage neither attached nor averse), or 2.70 (the ocean verse). 2.55-2.57 establish the steady-mind state without the four-fold disposition; 2.70 names the ocean metaphor not the four-fold disposition. The 'this is MY code' engineering pathology is anchored in the privative-prefix compounds (nir-mama, nir-ahaṅkāra) which are 2.71's specific contribution. Pass.
